primary.notes: Single-draft calibration fixture limits LI-6 to a degenerate evaluation; flagged in rationale, scored neutral-with-uplift rather than penalized. Structural pass: 785 chars in SHORT_TAKE [500,900]; 3 hashtags in [3,5]. Grounding pass: lived-work entity 'gofreddy' named (clears HARD FLOOR), explicit attribution line for the 149/25/9 lens counts, INTERPRETIVE close framed as JR's lesson. Two recurring caps: 'Lesson:' label-close pulls LI-1 and LI-4 a notch each; the 'three substantively different ways across three pages' detail is the one specific claim a LinkedIn buyer could not independently fact-check, capping LI-2.

per_criterion.rationale: Thoughtful-authority register throughout. First-person, story-led: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks watching...' / 'By the third day I realized...'. Plain language — the one jargon spike ('149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames') is anchored by the inline plain-English follow-up 'lens catalog we ship to clients'. Tone is 'here's what I noticed' not 'you're doing this wrong'. Not Twitter-translated — no contrarian declarative, no sub-200-char aggressive sharps. Minor slip: the 'Lesson:' label-close is slightly formulaic, which keeps this from a clean 9-10.

SOURCE-style claims (149/25/9 lens counts) carry an explicit attribution line ('gofreddy lens catalog v2 locked 2026-04-23'). Lived-work claim names a real entity ('gofreddy') — clears the HARD FLOOR. INTERPRETIVE close ('good enough to ship') is framed as JR's lesson, not declared fact. The vague-specific risk: 'About page describes the company in three substantively different ways across three pages' is a first-person specific claim about gofreddy's own pages — entity is named so the LinkedIn vague-specificity cap doesn't trigger, but the three-page detail is not independently verifiable from artifacts; that keeps this from a clean 8-9. Cannot cross-check voice.md from artifacts.

Opening sentence is 110 chars, story-led, concretely grounded: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch.' The 'finish before lunch' detail does real work — it's a specific lived-work signal that earns line two. First two sentences combined sit ~205 chars, inside the ~210 desktop show-more reference. Sentence two ('By the third day I realized it was scoring my own positioning, line by line.') delivers genuine before-the-fold tension — the audit turning back on its author. Not contrarian-bait, no engagement-bait close-style opener. Held below 9 because the hook is competent rather than memorable; no surprising image or stake escalation.

No 'Thoughts? 👇', no engagement-bait emoji prompt, no 'Here's what I learned.' alone-line close, no 'Three takeaways:' framing, no whitespace inflation (4 paragraphs with single-line breaks — structural, not visual padding). One LinkedIn-AI-adjacent slip: 'Lesson:' alone-line close is a label-close pattern that LinkedIn-AI drafts lean on, even though the lesson itself is substantive. The 'Not in a "look at this dashboard" way' aside is JR-cadence, not AI-cadence. Cap at 7 due to the single 'Lesson:' formulaic transition; would be 9 with a more naturally-cadenced close.

char_count 785 sits cleanly inside SHORT_TAKE (500-900). Structure earns the length: story-opening (P1) → escalation specific (P2) → brand-context anchor with verifiable numbers (P3) → implication close (P4). No pad-to-length filler — each paragraph carries distinct weight. Hashtag count = 3 (B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents), inside the ideal [3,5] band, no cap-at-7 trigger, and the tags map to the declared B2B-marketing voice_pillar rather than the generic '#marketing' floor. Held below 9 because SHORT_TAKE doesn't stress-test structural mastery the way THOUGHT_LEADER/CASE_STUDY brackets do.

Cohort N=1 — the calibration-short_take fixture supplies a single draft, so cross-item archetype variance and pillar spread are not meaningfully evaluable. The single draft fits its declared archetype (story-led) cleanly and matches its declared voice_pillar (B2B-marketing), so per-draft cohort-fit is positive; the geometric mean of one positive observation lands mid-range rather than at the 5 floor. Scoring at 6 to reflect 'neutral with slight uplift for archetype-fit' without rewarding diversity that cannot be demonstrated. A multi-draft cohort would be required to score 8+.

secondary.notes: Strong LinkedIn short-take shape and opener, but grounding cannot pass without the required voice.md artifact, and the draft uses unexplained internal jargon.

per_criterion.rationale: The draft is first-person and story-led: "Last quarter I spent two weeks..." and "By the third day I realized..." It has a thoughtful-authority register rather than a contrarian X-style hook. However, jargon appears without plain-English follow-up: "149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames" and "lens catalog" are not explained for a B2B LinkedIn buyer, so this dimension is capped.

The draft makes specific lived-work claims: "watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch," "the same lens catalog we ship to clients," and the specific counts "149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames." The named entity is gofreddy, but no programs/references/voice.md artifact is provided, so the required voice.md grounding cannot be verified from what is in front of me. The claims are internally attributed in META, but not externally grounded.

The opener is story-led and concrete: "Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch." It gives time, actor, project context, and a result before the fold. It earns the next line without a hot-take frame.

No obvious LinkedIn-AI tells appear. There is no "Thoughts?" close, no emoji bait, no "Here's what I learned" standalone cadence, and paragraph breaks serve structure. The closing "Lesson: when your audit is good enough..." is a little formulaic, but not enough to read as AI-LinkedIn slop.

For SHORT_TAKE, the structure fits: story opening, substantive middle, clear closing thought, and 3 targeted hashtags in META: "B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents." The length is earned by specific detail rather than padding. Hashtags map to the topic and brand area.

Only one draft is provided in the session artifacts, so cohort variation across story-led, lesson-led, comparison, and case-study cannot be demonstrated. The single available draft is story-led and uses one visible pillar, "B2B-marketing." Scoring only what is in front of me, the cohort is too small to show the requested diversity.
